accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and the contextual claim that the Secretary is the Secretary of Energy is verified as correct (program administered by DOE).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the added context (Secretary of Energy) is accurate and consistent with DOE administration of the program.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose as reauthorizing the Rural and Municipal Utility Advanced Cybersecurity Grant and Technical Assistance Program.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly states the bill amends/replaces Section 40124 of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes eligibility limits (e.g., investor-owned utilities under 4 million MWh), priority criteria, and the FOIA exemption carve-out.

effective_dates.why: Notes the appropriations period of fiscal years 2026 through 2030.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures eligible entities, awards mechanism, objectives, priorities, information protection, and authorization amount.

objective_tone.why: Summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

concise.why: No padding or repetition; appropriately compact.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the IIJA, Section 40124, FOIA, the bulk-power system, and accurately identifies the Secretary of Energy.

correct_figures.why: The 4,000,000 MWh threshold, $250,000,000 authorization, FY2026-2030 period, and six-entity partnership figure all match the bill.
